text: Agents can now spend money and provision tools.
Here the mission is to resolve one hundred late-freight exceptions in seventy-two hours.
Agent IC gives them a governed capital account.
Hermes and Nemotron load the intake skill, normalize the proposal, and score viability.
OpenShell creates a sandbox around the agent, scopes the network policy, and records the invariants.
OpenShell intercepts the call and returns four-oh-three Forbidden.
Stripe records a bounded one-hundred-dollar authorization.
The Stripe Link skill can buy a fifteen-dollar SaaS subscription with a one-time card, but only after human approval.
The agent tries to buy a one-hundred-fifty-dollar premium lookup API.
Then the agent imports evidence: one hundred cases processed, forty-eight auto-triaged, ninety-one percent QA agreement.
Within the remaining envelope, Hermes skills auto-order replacement parts and notify the team in Slack.
The decision is continue, with a two-hundred-fifty-dollar next cap and draft-only autonomy.
Hermes saves the whole procedure as a reusable playbook.
Lower the QA threshold to eighty-two percent, and the decision flips to kill.
This is how enterprises let agents operate with money without losing control.
